author: John Chrysostom
book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.

ccel.title: NPNF1-10. St. Chrysostom: Homilies on the Gospel of Saint Matthew

source.title: NPNF1-10. St. Chrysostom: Homilies on the Gospel of Saint Matthew

title: Matthew XXIII. 1-3.

source.title: NPNF1-10. St. Chrysostom: Homilies on the Gospel of Saint Matthew

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “Then spake Jesus to the multitudes and to His disciples, saying, The Scribes and the Pharisees sit in Moses’ seat: all therefore whatsoever they bid you do, that do;but do not after their works.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: When He had said these things, when He had stopped their mouths; when He had brought them that they should no more dare to tempt Him; when He had shown their state incurable.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And since He had made mention of “the Lord” and “my Lord,” He recurs again to the law.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And yet the law said nothing of this kind, but, “The Lord thy God is one Lord.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But Scripture calls the whole Old Testament the law.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But these things He saith, showing by all things His full agreement with Him that begat Him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For if He were opposed, He would have said the opposite about the law; but now He commands so great reverence to be shown towards it, that, even when they that teach it are depraved, He charges them to hold to it.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But here He is discoursing about their life and morals, since this was chiefly the cause of their unbelief, their depraved life, and the love of glory.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: To amend therefore His hearers; that which in the first place most contributes to salvation, not to despise our teachers, neither to rise up against our priests, this doth He command with superabundant earnestness.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But He does not only command it, but also Himself doth it.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For though they were depraved, He doth not depose them from their dignity; to them rendering their condemnation heavier, and to His disciples leaving no cloke for disobedience.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: I mean, that lest any one should say, that because my teacher is bad, therefore am I become more remiss, He takes away even this pretext.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: So much at any rate did He establish their authority, although they were wicked men, as even after so heavy an accusation to say, “All whatsoever they command you to do, do.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For they speak not their own words, but God’s, what He appointed for laws by Moses.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And mark how much honor He showed towards Moses, again showing His agreement with the Old Testament; since indeed even by this doth He make them objects of reverence.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “For they sit,” He saith, “on Moses’ seat.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For because He was not able to make them out worthy of credit by their life, He doth it from the grounds that were open to Him, from their seat, and their succession from him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But when thou hearest all, do not understand all the law, as, for instance, the ordinances about meats, those about sacrifices, and the like; for how was He to say so of these things, which He had taken away beforehand? but He meant all things that correct the moral principle, and amend the disposition, and agree with the laws of the New Testament, and suffer them not any more to be under the yoke of the law.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Wherefore then doth He give these things divine authority, not from the law of grace, but from Moses?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Because it was not yet time, before the crucifixion, for these things to be plainly declared.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But to me He seems, in addition to what has been said, to be providing for another object, in saying these things.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For since He was on the point of accusing them, that He might not seem in the sight of the foolish to set His heart on this authority of theirs, or for enmity to be doing these things, first He removed this thought, and having set himself clear from suspicion, then begins His accusation.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And for what intent doth He convict them, and run out into a long discourse against them?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: To set the multitude on their guard, so that they might not fall into the same sins.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For neither is dissuading like pointing out those that have offended; much as recommending what is right, is not like bringing forward those that have done well.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For this cause also He is beforehand in saying, “Do not after their works.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For, lest they should suppose, because of their listening to them, they ought also to imitate them, He uses this means of correction, and makes what seems to be their dignity a charge against them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For what can be more wretched than a teacher, when the preservation of his disciples is, not to give heed to his life?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: So that what seemeth to be their dignity is a most heavy charge against them, when they are shown to live such a life, as they that imitate are ruined.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For this cause He also falls upon His accusations against them, but not for this only, but that He might show, that both their former unbelief wherewith they had not believed, and the crucifixion after this, which they dared to perpetrate, were not a charge against Him who was crucified and disbelieved, but against their perverseness.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But see whence He begins, and whence He aggravates His blame of them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “For they say,” He saith, “and do not.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For every one is worthy of blame in transgressing the law, but especially he that bears the authority of teaching, for doubly and triply doth he deserve to be condemned.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For one cause, because he transgresses; for another, that as he ought to amend others, and then halteth, he is worthy of a double punishment, because of his dignity; and in the third place, that he even corrupts the more, as committing such transgression in a teacher’s place.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And together with these He mentions also another charge against them, that they are harsh to those accountable to them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “For they bind heavy burdens, and grievous to be borne, and lay them on men’s shoulders, but they will not move them with their finger.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: He mentions here a twofold wickedness, their requiring great and extreme strictness of life, without any indulgence, from those over whom they rule, and their allowing to themselves great security; the opposite to which the truly good ruler ought to hold; in what concerns himself, to be an unpardoning and severe judge, but in the matters of those whom he rules, to be gentle and ready to make allowances; the contrary to which was the conduct of these men.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For such are all they who practise self restraint in mere words, unpardoning and grievous to bear as having no experience of the difficulty in actions.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And this itself too is no small fault, and in no ordinary way increases the former charge.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But do thou mark, I pray thee, how He aggravates this accusation also.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For He did not say, “they cannot,” but, “they will not.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And He did not say, “to bear,” but, “to move with a finger,” that is, not even to come near them, nor to touch them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But wherein are they earnest, and vigorous?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: In the things forbidden.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For, “all their works they do,” He saith, “to be seen of men.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: These things He saith, accusing them in respect of vainglory, which kind of thing was their ruin.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For the things before were signs of harshness and remissness, but these of the mad desire of glory.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: This drew them off from God, this caused them to strive before other spectators, and ruined them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For whatever kind of spectators any one may have, since it hath become his study to please these, such also are the contests he exhibits.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And he that wrestles among the noble, such also are the conflicts he takes in hand, but he among the cold and supine, himself also becomes more remiss.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For instance, hath any one a beholder that delights in ridicule? he himself too becomes a mover of ridicule, that he may delight the spectator: hath another one who is earnest minded, and practises self-government? he endeavors himself to be such as he is, since such is the disposition of him who praises him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But see again that here too the charge is with aggravation.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For neither is it that they do some things in this way, some in another way, but all things absolutely this way.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Then, having blamed them for vainglory, He shows that it is not even about great and necessary things they are vainglorious (for neither had they these, but were destitute of good works), but for things without warmth or worth, and such as were certain proofs of their baseness, the phylacteries, the borders; of their garments.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “For they make broad their phylacteries,” He saith, “and enlarge the borders of their garments.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And what are these phylacteries, and these borders?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Since they were continually forgetting God’s benefits, He commanded His marvellous works to be inscribed on little tablets, and that these should be suspended from their hands (wherefore also He said, “They shall be immoveable in thine eyes”),which they called phylacteries; as many of our women now wear Gospels hung from their necks.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And in order that by another thing again they may be reminded, like as many often do, binding round their finger with a piece of linen or a thread, as being likely to forget, this God enjoined them as children to do, “to sew a ribbon of blue on their garments, upon the fringe that hung round their feet, that they might look at it, and remember the commandments;”and they were called “borders.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: In these things then they were diligent, making wide the strips of the tablets, and enlarging the borders of their garments; which was a sign of the most extreme vanity.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For wherefore art thou vainglorious, and dost make these wide? what, is this thy good work? what doth it profit thee at all, if thou gain not the good results from them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For God seeks not the enlarging of these and making them wide, but our remembering His benefits.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But if for almsgiving and prayer, although they be attended with labor, and be good deeds on our parts, we must not seek vainglory, how dost thou, O Jew, pride thyself in these things, which most of all convict thy remissness.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But they not in these only, but in other little things, suffered from this disease.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For, “they love,” He saith, “the uppermost roomsat feasts, and the chief seats in the synagogues, and greetings in the markets, and to be called of men, Rabbi.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For these things, although one may think them small, yet are they a cause of great evils.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: These things have overthrown both cities and churches.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And it comes upon me now even to weep, when I hear of the first seats, and the greetings, and consider how many ills were hence engendered to the churches of God, which it is not necessary to publish to you now; nay rather as many as are aged men do not even need to learn these things from us.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But mark thou, I pray thee, how vainglory prevailed; when they were commanded not to be vainglorious, even in the synagogues, where they had entered to discipline others.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For to have this feeling at feasts, to howsoever great a degree, doth not seem to be so dreadful a thing; although even there the teachers ought to be held in reverence, and not in the church only, but everywhere.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And like as a man, wherever he may appear, is manifestly distinguished from the brutes; so also ought the teacher, both speaking and holding his peace, and dining, and doing whatever it may be, to be distinguished as well by his gait, as by his look, and by his garb, and by all things generally.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But they were on every account objects of ridicule, and in every respect disgraced themselves, making it their study to follow what they ought to flee.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For they love them, it is said; but if the loving them be a matter of blame, what a thing must the doing them be; and to hunt and strive after them, how great an evil.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: The other things then He carried no further than to accuse them, as being small and trifling, and as though His disciples needed not at all to be corrected about these matters; but what was a cause of all the evils, even ambition, and the violent seizing of the teacher’s chair, this He brings forward, and corrects with diligence, touching this vehemently and earnestly charging them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For what saith He?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “But be not ye called Rabbi.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Then follows the cause also; “For one is your master, and all ye are brethren;”and one hath nothing more than another, in respect of his knowing nothing from himself.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Wherefore Paul also saith, “For who is Paul, and who is Apollos, but ministers?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: He said not masters.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And again, “Call not, father,”not that they should not call, but they may know whom they ought to call Father, in the highest sense.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For like as the master is not a master principally; so neither is the father.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For He is cause of all, both of the masters, and of the fathers.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And again He adds, “Neither be ye called guides, for one is your guide, even Christ;”and He said not, I.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For like as above He said, “What think ye of Christ?”and He said not, “of me,” so here too.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But I should be glad to ask here, what they would say, who are repeatedly applying the term one, one, to the Father alone, to the rejection of the Only-begotten.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Is the Father guide?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: All would declare it, and none would gainsay it.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And yet “one,” He saith, “is your guide, even Christ.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For like as Christ, being called the one guide, casts not out the Father from being guide; even so the Father, being called Master, doth not cast out the Son from being Master.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For the expression, one, one, is spoken in contradistinction to men, and the rest of the creation.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Having warned them therefore against this grievous pest, and amended them, He instructs also how they may escape it; by humility.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Wherefore He adds also, “He that is greatest among you shall be your servant.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For whosoever shall exalt himself shall be abased, and whosoever shall abase himself shall be exalted.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For nothing is equal to the practice of modesty, wherefore He is continually reminding them of this virtue, both when He brought the children into the midst, and now.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And, when on the mount, beginning the beatitudes, He began from hence.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And in this place, He plucks it up by the roots hereby, saying, “He that abaseth himself shall be exalted.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Seest thou how He draws off the hearer right over to the contrary thing.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For not only doth He forbid him to set his heart upon the first place, but requires him to follow after the last.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For so shalt thou obtain thy desire, He saith.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Wherefore he that pursues his desire for the first, must follow after the last place.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “For he that abaseth himself shall be exalted.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And where shall we find this humility?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Will ye that we go again to the city of virtue, the tents of the holy men, the mountains, I mean, and the groves?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For there too shall we see this height of humility.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For men, some illustrious from their rank in the world, some from their wealth, in every way put themselves down, by their vesture, by their dwelling, by those to whom they minister; and, as in written characters, they throughout all things inscribe humility.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And the things that are incentives of arrogance, as to dress well, and to build houses splendidly, and to have many servants, things which often drive men even against their will to arrogance; these are all taken away.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For they themselves light their fire, they themselves cleave the logs, themselves cook, themselves minister to those that come there.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: No one can be heard insulting there, nor seen insulted, nor commanded, nor giving commands; but all are devoted to those that are waited on, and every one washes the strangers’ feet, and there is much contention about this.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And he doeth it, not inquiring who it is, neither if he be a slave, nor if he be free; but in the case of every one fulfills this service.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: No man there is great nor mean.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Is there confusion?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Far from it, but the highest order.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For if any one be mean, he that is great seeth not this, but hath accounted himself again to be inferior even to him, and so becomes great.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: There is one table for all, both for them that are served, and for them that serve; the same food, the same clothes, the same dwellings, the same manner of life.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: He is great there, who eagerly seizes the mean task.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: There is not mine and thine, but this expression is exterminated, that is a cause of countless wars.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And why dost thou marvel, if there be one manner of life and table and dress for all, since indeed there is even one soul to all, not in substance only (for this is with all men also), but in love? how then should it ever be lifted up itself against itself?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: There is no wealth and poverty there, honor and dishonor; how then should haughtiness and arrogance find an entrance?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For they are indeed little and great in respect of their virtue; but, as I have said, no one seeth this.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: He that is little, feels not pain, as despised; for neither is there any one to despise him; and should any one spurn him, this above all are they taught, to be despised, to be spurned, to be set at nought, in word and in deed.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And with the poor and maimed do they associate, and their tables are full of these guests; so that for this are they worthy of the heavens.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And one tends the wounds of the mutilated, another leads the blind by the hand, a third bears him that is lamed of his leg.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: There is no multitude of flatterers or parasites there; or rather they know not even what flattery is; whence then could they be lifted up at any time?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For there is great equality amongst them, wherefore also there is much facility for virtue.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For by these are they of an inferior sort better instructed, than if they were compelled to give up the first place to them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For like as the impetuous man derives instruction from him that is smitten, and submits to it; so the ambitious from him that claims not glory, but despises it.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: This they do there abundantly, and as the strife is great with us to obtain the first place, so great is it with them not to obtain it, but utterly to refuse it; and great is their earnest desire who shall have the advantage in honoring, not in being honored.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And besides, even their very employments persuade them to practise moderation, and not to be high-swollen.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For who, I pray thee, digging in the earth, and watering, and planting, or making baskets, or weaving sackcloth, or practising any other handy works, will ever be proud?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Who dwelling in poverty and wrestling with hunger, will ever be sick of this disease?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: There is not one.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Therefore humility is easy to them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And like as here, it is a hard thing to be lowly minded, for the multitude of them who applaud and admire us, so there it is exceedingly easy.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And that man gives heed only to the wilderness, and sees birds flying, and trees waving, and a breeze blowing, and streams rushing through glens.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Whence then should he be lifted up who dwells in solitude so great?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Not however that therefore we have from this an excuse, in that we are proud when living in the midst of men.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For surely Abraham, when amidst Canaanites, said, “I am but dust and ashes;”and David, when in the midst of camps,“I am a worm, and no man;”and the apostle, in the midst of the world, “I am not meet to be called an apostle.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: What comfort shall we have then; what plea, when even, having such great examples, we do not practise moderation?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For even as they are worthy of countless crowns, having been the first that went the way of virtue, even so are we deserving of countless punishments, who not even after those that are departed, and are set before us in books, no nor even after these that are living, and held in admiration through their deeds, are drawn on to the like emulation.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For what couldest thou say, for not being amended?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Art thou ignorant of letters, and hast not looked into the Scriptures that thou mightest learn the virtues of them of old?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: To say the truth, this is itself blameworthy, when the church is constantly standing open, not to enter in, and partake of those sacred streams.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: However, although thou know not the departed by the Scriptures, these living men thou oughtest to see.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But is there no one to lead thee?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Come to me, and I will show thee the places of refuge of these holy men; come and learn thou of them something useful.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Shining lamps are these in every part of the earth; as walls are they set about the cities.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For this cause have they occupied the deserts, that they may instruct thee to despise the tumults in the midst of the world.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For they, as being strong, are able even in the midst of the raging of the waters to enjoy a calm; but thou, who art leaky on every side, hast need of tranquility, and to take breath a little, after the successive waves.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Go then there continually, that, having purged away the abiding stain by their prayers and admonitions, thou mayest both pass in the best manner the present life, and attain unto the good things to come, by the grace and love towards man of our Lord Jesus Christ, by whom and with whom, be unto the Father, together with the Holy Ghost, glory, might, honor, now and ever, and world without end.
